questionHtml:
What is the coefficient for H+ when the equation below is balanced?

__Pb (s) + __NO3− (aq) + __H+ (aq) → __Pb2+ (aq) + __NO (g) + __H2O (l)

 

A.  2

B.  4

C.  6

D.  8

meta.title: What is the coefficient for H+ when the equation below is balanced? __Pb (s) + __NO3− (aq) + __H+ (aq) → __Pb2+ (aq) + __NO (g) + __H2O (l) A. 2 B. 4 C. 6 D. 8
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 2. How much, how fast and how far?
Reactivity 2.1—How much? The amount of chemical change
Reactivity 2.1.1—Chemical equations show the ratio of reactants and products in a reaction. Deduce chemical equations when reactants and products are specified.
